purpose: Header for PreferencesWindow — the single unified settings window with a top-to-bottom 'signal flow' layout (INPUT → AUDIO CHAIN → OUTPUT → DEVICE SETTINGS). Plugin chain edits, bypass, and lane assignments are staged locally and committed via the onApply std::function callback; closing fires onClose so the owner (IconMenu) can persist device state and release the unique_ptr.
invariants: Constructor takes three parallel vectors — activeChain (PluginDescription), bypassStates (bool), laneStates (int) — and onApply receives the same three. Sizes must match: chain[i], bypass[i], lane[i] all describe plugin i.
Apply commits changes and keeps the window open (per the header comment). Close-button triggers onClose regardless of whether Apply was ever clicked.
refreshPluginChain is called by the owner to update the UI after an external chain change (e.g. background load completed) — the window owner must hold the window alive across these refreshes.
JUCE_DECLARE_NON_COPYABLE_WITH_LEAK_DETECTOR — std::function callbacks capture by-value and the window has identity, so copying is unsafe.
gotchas: onApply / onEditPlugin / onClose are std::function — captured by-value at construction. Any heap state captured by IconMenu must outlive the PreferencesWindow (it does, since IconMenu owns the unique_ptr).
The onApply signature changed when lanes were added in v4.0.0 (third laneStates vector). Older callers that constructed the window with the two-vector form will not compile against this header.
